mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns as economic expansion persists.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when RSAFS is rising over the prior month.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and economic contraction, leading to lower corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 2-month period
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances after UNRATE has risen by 0.3pp in 2 months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below inflation, real interest rates become negative, encouraging borrowing and risk-taking, which boosts equity prices over short horizons.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL year-over-year change (negative real rate)
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations where real rates are negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is below 5%, the labor market is tight; wage pressures squeeze margins and reduce earnings growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months when UNRATE is below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production signals supply disruption or demand weakness, foreshadowing lower corporate output and negative equity returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 1% month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months following a 1%+ INDPRO decline.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous year-over-year growth in retail sales and month-over-month growth in payroll employment indicates synchronized economic strength, sustaining earnings momentum and lifting S&P 500 over a quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of months when both RSAFS and PAYEMS are positive.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment where the federal funds rate is below 2%, monetary accommodation lowers discount rates and supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect robust construction activity, wealth effects from home values, and positive sentiment, which boosts equity prices over short-term horizons.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of periods when HOUST is rising month-over-month.
